work_unit: Full deep audit of core layer (bff_client, runtime, duckdb_query, write_api, intent_planner) + sql-execution/execute_sql.py
situation: audit-cycle-state was cycle-complete after audit-037 (shizi). All git workspace projects audited ≥1×. Selected skills/dataworks-dev-assistant as next target — live copy at ~/.claude/skills/ with 115 executable Python files, sensitive paths (DuckDB/BFF/SQL exec/session/auth), never deep-audited before.
action: Deep audit reading 6 core files. Identified 5 findings: DW-CORE-H-001 (BFF log PII leak), DW-CORE-M-001 (non-atomic session_state writes in 2 functions), DW-SQL-M-001 (WITH-prefix CTE bypasses write confirm — same class as autonomous-studio e37253f fix), DW-CORE-L-001 (session_state lock contention structural debt), DW-CORE-I-001 (intent_planner clean). Wrote audit report audit-2026-07-08-038.md. Queued SD-018 to structural-debt.md. Updated audit-cycle-state to fix-in-progress with 3 pending route-fixes.
outcome_evidence: audit-2026-07-08-038.md written with 5 findings (1H/2M/1L/1I); audit-cycle-state.json updated status=fix-in-progress with 3 derived_fixes status=pending; SD-018 appended to structural-debt.md; no opt-worktree commit (audit-only round per constraints).
next_suggestion: Dispatch DW-CORE-H-001 route-fix: add response.data redaction to bff_client._log() (mask email/account/displayName/baseId/token keys)
After H-001 merged, dispatch DW-CORE-M-001: convert save_tool_result + _write_session_state to tmp+os.replace atomic pattern
After M-001 merged, dispatch DW-SQL-M-001: strip string literals then scan for write keywords in execute_sql._is_readonly_sql

audit_findings.finding: _log() writes full response.data to dw_bff_calls.log without redaction — may contain user PII (email/account/displayName/baseId) or query result data
audit_findings.remediation: Add recursive redaction of known sensitive keys in response.data before logging; or log only type+length for data field

audit_findings.finding: save_tool_result and _write_session_state use non-atomic open('w')+json.dump for session_state.json; concurrent agents can lose updates; crash truncates JSON. bootstrap_context (line 180-183) already uses correct tmp+os.replace pattern
audit_findings.remediation: Convert both functions to tmp+os.replace atomic write pattern matching bootstrap_context

audit_findings.finding: _is_readonly_sql matches WITH prefix as readonly, but WITH...INSERT/UPDATE/DELETE is valid write SQL that bypasses two-phase confirm. Same bug class as autonomous-studio commit e37253f
audit_findings.remediation: Strip string literals/comments then scan for INSERT/UPDATE/DELETE/MERGE/DROP/CREATE/ALTER/TRUNCATE keywords after WITH prefix match

audit_findings.finding: 5 writers share session_state.json (save_tool_result, _write_session_state, bootstrap_context, remember, forget) with no file locking — last-writer-wins under parallel agent dispatch
audit_findings.remediation: Introduce fcntl.flock or split into per-key state files (structural debt SD-018)
